title: Mildly Peppery Argentinian New Potato Salad
description: A light and refreshing Argentinian salad featuring new potatoes, with a mild peppery flavor and a crunchy texture, perfect for a snack or side dish.
instructions: Place the diced new potatoes in a large pot and add enough cold water to cover them.
Bring the water to a boil over high heat, then reduce the heat to medium-low and simmer for 10 minutes, or until the potatoes are tender when pierced with a fork.
Drain the potatoes and let them cool to room temperature.
In a large bowl, whisk together the lime juice, olive oil, salt, black pepper, and paprika.
Add the diced red bell pepper, thinly sliced red onion, chopped parsley, and chopped cilantro to the bowl and stir to combine.
Once the potatoes have cooled, add them to the bowl and gently fold them into the dressing.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate the salad for at least 30 minutes to allow the flavors to meld.
Just before serving, give the salad a good stir and adjust the seasoning if necessary.
Transfer the salad to a terracotta dish and serve chilled.
